intro: Braintree and Modern Treasury both show up under the payment gateway category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Braintree uses usage-based pricing, while Modern Treasury runs on custom (sales-led) pricing. Both sit at moderate lock-in. Transparency lands at 5/5 versus 1/5. Braintree fits teams working on SaaS with online payments, while Modern Treasury is a closer match when the job is ACH, wire, and RTP payment operations with real-time ledgering API. Worth noting: Braintree is explicitly not for offline, Internal projects; Modern Treasury is explicitly not for merchants who mainly need card acceptance. The honest trade-off: Braintree trades off on offline-only retail; Modern Treasury trades off on US-bank-focused, limited globally. On the plus side, Braintree highlights SaaS with online payments, while Modern Treasury points to deep ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow coverage. Braintree's documentation also calls out e-commerce checkout. Modern Treasury similarly notes real-time ledgering built in. For teams evaluating payment gateway options today, the right choice usually comes down to pricing model, lock-in, and which tool's stated use-case maps to yours.
quick_take: Braintree is for SaaS with online payments; Modern Treasury is for ACH, wire, and RTP payment operations; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Braintree if your project is SaaS with online payments, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.
verdict_b: Choose Modern Treasury if your project is ACH, wire, and RTP payment operations with real-time ledgering API, you are prepared for a sales-led custom-pricing conversation, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.

faq.q: Is Braintree cheaper than Modern Treasury?
faq.a: Braintree uses usage-based pricing, and Modern Treasury uses custom (sales-led) pricing. The pricing models are different, so a direct cheaper-than comparison depends on your volume and usage pattern.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Braintree to Modern Treasury?
faq.a: Our data puts Braintree at medium lock-in (paypal-owned, card data portable), and Modern Treasury at medium lock-in (payment ops saas, custom integrations). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.
Both score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. Braintree does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (5/5 vs 1/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is Modern Treasury a good alternative to Braintree?
faq.a: Modern Treasury is a reasonable alternative to Braintree when your workload leans more toward platforms and fintechs running high-volume bank transfers and ledgering in the US than SaaS, E-commerce, Marketplace. The pricing model shifts too — Braintree is usage-based pricing, Modern Treasury is custom (sales-led) pricing — so expect the cost profile to change as well. One caveat: Modern Treasury is explicitly not for merchants who mainly need card acceptance, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
